schema.name: CiscoUmbrellaRemoteAccessVPN_CL
schema.description: The Cisco Secure Access remote access virtual private network (VPN) logs show the VPN session connection events, which are managed by the Secure Access VPN services.

columns.name: Timestamp
columns.description: The date and time of the RAVPN event, expressed as a UTC-formatted string.

columns.name: HostName
columns.description: The name of the node on the Cisco headend side where user VPN connections are established and events are generated.

columns.name: AwsRegion
columns.description: The AWS region that stores your VPN logs.

columns.name: EventType
columns.description: The label that describes the type of event. Valid values are: CONNECTED, DISCONNECTED, FAILED, or UNKNOWN.

columns.name: OriginIds
columns.description: The internal IP address of the device that connected to the Secure Access remote VPN services.

columns.name: OriginType
columns.description: The type of device connected to the Secure Access VPN services.

columns.name: UserId
columns.description: The ID of the VPN user. The ID is the email address associated with the user account.

columns.name: OrganizationId
columns.description: The Secure Access organization ID.

columns.name: RetentionDays
columns.description: The number of days that AWS S3 stores your Secure Access VPN log.

columns.name: StorageLocation
columns.description: The two-character label that identifies the location of your Cisco-managed VPN logs. Configure the storage location on Secure Access for your organization. The storage location options are: eu or us.

columns.name: MspOrganizationId
columns.description: The Secure Access managed organization ID.

columns.name: SessionId
columns.description: The unique ID of the VPN session.

columns.name: SessionType
columns.description: The protocol used by the device with the VPN session, for example: TLS.

columns.name: VpnProfile
columns.description: The name of the VPN connection profile that establishes a VPN session.

columns.name: PublicIp
columns.description: The public IP address of the device with the Cisco Secure Client and VPN module.

columns.name: AssignedIp
columns.description: The IP address assigned to the device with the Cisco Secure Client and VPN module.

columns.name: ConnectedAt
columns.description: The date and time of the start of the initial CONNECTED VPN event for a DISCONNECTED event expressed in milliseconds as a UTC-formatted string.

columns.name: DisconnectionReason
columns.description: The description of the VPN disconnected event. The value is null for other event types.

columns.name: OsVersion
columns.description: The type and version of the user device's operating system.

columns.name: AnyConnectVersion
columns.description: The version of the Cisco Secure Client with the VPN module.

columns.name: AsaSyslogId
columns.description: The ID of the Cisco ASA syslog used to generate this log event.

columns.name: DeviceId
columns.description: The ID of the device with the Cisco Secure Client and VPN module.

columns.name: MachineId
columns.description: The ID of the client machine used for authentication.

columns.name: PublicIpv6
columns.description: The public IP v6 address of the device with the Cisco Secure Client and VPN module.

columns.name: AssignedIpv6
columns.description: The IP v6 address assigned to the device with the Cisco Secure Client and VPN module.

columns.name: SecurityGroupTag
columns.description: Security group tag matched as a source by a rule.

columns.name: DapRecordName
columns.description: The posture profile assessed by Cisco Secure Client HostScan.

columns.name: DapConnectionType
columns.description: The RAVPN session connection type.

columns.name: FailedReasons
columns.description: The error codes for failed remote connection requests.

columns.name: LogMessage
columns.description: Log message details communications between devices involved in 802.1X port-based network access. This message is used to troubleshoot issues with authentication, authorization, and accounting (AAA) processes, including RADIUS servers.

columns.name: AsaSyslogClass
columns.description: The syslog message class categorizes syslog messages by type, representing a device's feature or function.

columns.name: AsaSyslogDescriptor
columns.description: The syslog message format typically includes a timestamp, a hostname or IP address, facility code, severity level, and the actual log message content, all of which are used for system logging and analysis.
